generatedSentence: At a vibrant vernissage, the goosy journalist, lately sensing a critical lack, decided to polish an old-fashioned story for a better ratio. He worked with the venue manager, a genial human who ran a funky house on an immersive island, hoping to produce a kicky piece. He guessed an easy, relaxing interview, complete with a fluffy horse and a bonjour, might just be the element of humor needed to defend his journalistic integrity. However, the peace was fleeting; a massive trijets airburst signaled mayhem, causing the crowd to jabber with perplexity. A manticore, an ontological entity, descended from the djebels, its form a wavering menace. Its lackey, a buffoonish gremlin, began to heckle and kick down the elaborate storage units, causing a titanic racket. The journalist, whose temper now flared, watched as the monster began to dredge the liquid gold from the house's foundation, creating a swirling whirlpool of chaos. The princess, arriving via helicopter with her uncle, prepared to dodge the entity's attacks. Her old friend, a majestic jaguar, wore a caparison and looked ready to fight, but the sight of the kinky knots the manticore fluffed in its fur made him fidget. The journalist, observing the gaffing manager and the group of hippies in the junkyard, knew he couldn't just carp. He, a true believer, had to act, even if it meant risking everything, as the beast's booming voice made the very necks of the crowd go goosy. With a whistle and a swift move, the journalist, now smiling, used an esoteric chess move to trap the manticore, causing its gremlin to farting in a giddy rage. He watched as the creature's weaker hold on the underworld gridlock was broken, allowing a cascade of coconuts from the blown-up jetways. Saying goodbye, the journalist felt a profound love for the wholesome victory. He had not only saved the day from the blowfish-like monster but also secured a preview for his next film, knowing the story was golden, even if he felt a bit underage for such heroics.

He worked with the venue manager, a genial human who ran a funky house on an immersive island, hoping to produce a kicky piece. He guessed an easy, relaxing interview, complete with a fluffy horse and a bonjour, might just be the element of humor needed to defend his journalistic integrity.

However, the peace was fleeting; a massive trijets airburst signaled mayhem, causing the crowd to jabber with perplexity. A manticore, an ontological entity, descended from the djebels, its form a wavering menace. Its lackey, a buffoonish gremlin, began to heckle and kick down the elaborate storage units, causing a titanic racket. The journalist, whose temper now flared, watched as the monster began to dredge the liquid gold from the house's foundation, creating a swirling whirlpool of chaos.

The princess, arriving via helicopter with her uncle, prepared to dodge the entity's attacks. Her old friend, a majestic jaguar, wore a caparison and looked ready to fight, but the sight of the kinky knots the manticore fluffed in its fur made him fidget. The journalist, observing the gaffing manager and the group of hippies in the junkyard, knew he couldn't just carp. He, a true believer, had to act, even if it meant risking everything, as the beast's booming voice made the very necks of the crowd go goosy.

With a whistle and a swift move, the journalist, now smiling, used an esoteric chess move to trap the manticore, causing its gremlin to farting in a giddy rage. He watched as the creature's weaker hold on the underworld gridlock was broken, allowing a cascade of coconuts from the blown-up jetways. He had not only saved the day from the blowfish-like monster but also secured a preview for his next film, knowing the story was golden, even if he felt a bit underage for such heroics.
imagePrompt: A professional 4-panel comic book page, 2x2 grid, sequential art, vibrant neon futurism, bold ink outlines, cinematic lighting, thick white gutters, clean black borders. Protagonist: A determined journalist with a vintage camera, wearing a trench coat and a fedora. TOP-LEFT PANEL: The journalist stands amidst a neon-lit, futuristic art vernissage, observing a fluffy horse interacting with guests, eye-level shot. TOP-RIGHT PANEL: A monstrous manticore descends from glowing red djebels, causing an airburst, with a buffoonish gremlin on its back kicking down storage units, wide shot. BOTTOM-LEFT PANEL: A princess, with her uncle, on a helicopter, ready to dodge the manticore's chaotic dredge, while a majestic jaguar in a caparison readies to spring, medium shot. BOTTOM-RIGHT PANEL: The smiling journalist, holding a vintage camera, watches the manticore caught in kinky knots, surrounded by scattered coconuts, as jetways crumble in the background, close-up shot.

definitions.definition: A number representing a comparison between two named things.
The relative magnitudes of two quantities (usually expressed as a quotient).
Short for ratio decidendi.
The amount of comments to a post or other expression on social media relative to the number of likes.
(social media) To respond to a post or message on social media in a greater number than the number of likes the post receives.
definitions.example: The schoolchildren ganged up to ratio the school catering over its shamefully undemocratic inclusion of vegetables in meals.

definitions.definition: A theater, auditorium, arena, or other area designated for sporting or entertainment events.
A neighborhood or near place; the place or county in which anything is alleged to have happened; also, the place where an action is laid, or the district from which a jury comes.
A bout; a hit; a turn. See venew.
Sport venue: a stadium or similar building in which a sporting competition is held.
A private viewing of an art exhibition before it opens to the public.
A goose.
A foolish person; a silly.
Characteristic of a goose; anserine
Foolish; silly
Along, together with others, in a group, etc.
definitions.example: Do you want to come with?

definitions.definition: Against.
definitions.example: He picked a fight with the class bully.

definitions.definition: In the company of; alongside, close to; near to.
definitions.example: He went with his friends.

definitions.definition: In addition to; as an accessory to.
definitions.example: She owns a motorcycle with a sidecar.

definitions.definition: Used to indicate simultaneous happening, or immediate succession or consequence.
definitions.example: Jim was listening to Bach with his eyes closed.

definitions.definition: In support of.
definitions.example: We are with you all the way.

definitions.definition: In regard to.
definitions.example: He was pleased with the outcome.

definitions.definition: To denote the accomplishment of cause, means, instrument, etc; – sometimes equivalent to by.
definitions.example: slain with robbers

definitions.definition: Using as an instrument; by means of.
definitions.example: Find what you want instantly with our search engine.

definitions.definition: Using as nourishment; more recently replaced by on.
Having, owning.
definitions.example: It was small and bumpy, with a tinge of orange.

definitions.definition: Affected by (a certain emotion or condition).
definitions.example: He spoke with sadness in his voice.

definitions.definition: Prompted by (a certain emotion).
definitions.example: green with envy; flushed with success

definitions.definition: A cocktail made by muddling sugar with bitters and adding whiskey or, less commonly, brandy, served with a twist of citrus rind.
Of a thing, outdated or no longer in vogue.
definitions.example: My bike is old-fashioned but it gets me around.

definitions.definition: Of a person, preferring the customs of earlier times.
definitions.example: You can’t stay the night, because my parents are a bit old-fashioned.

definitions.definition: Lively, exciting, thrilling.
Characterised by kicking motions.
definitions.example: a kicky dance routine

definitions.definition: Describing a wicket that sometimes causes the ball to kick (bounce unevenly).
More than half (50%) of some group.
definitions.example: The majority agreed that the new proposal was the best.

definitions.definition: The difference between the winning vote and the rest of the votes.
definitions.example: The winner with 53% had a 6% majority over the loser with 47%.

definitions.definition: Legal adulthood.
definitions.example: By the time I reached my majority, I had already been around the world twice.

definitions.definition: The office held by a member of the armed forces in the rank of major.
definitions.example: On receiving the news of his promotion, Charles Snodgrass said he was delighted to be entering his majority.

definitions.definition: Ancestors; ancestry.
A substance used to polish.
definitions.example: A good silver polish will remove tarnish easily.

definitions.definition: Cleanliness; smoothness, shininess.
definitions.example: The floor was waxed to a high polish.

definitions.definition: Refinement; cleanliness in performance or presentation.
definitions.example: The lecturer showed a lot of polish at his last talk.

definitions.definition: To shine; to make a surface very smooth or shiny by rubbing, cleaning, or grinding.
definitions.example: He polished up the chrome until it gleamed.

definitions.definition: To refine; remove imperfections from.
definitions.example: The band has polished its performance since the last concert.

definitions.definition: To apply shoe polish to shoes.
To become smooth, as from friction; to receive a gloss; to take a smooth and glossy surface.
definitions.example: Steel polishes well.

definitions.definition: To refine; to wear off the rudeness, coarseness, or rusticity of; to make elegant and polite.
Recently; not long ago; of late.
A defect or failing; moral or spiritual degeneracy.
A deficiency or need (of something desirable or necessary); an absence, want.
To be without, to need, to require.
definitions.example: My life lacks excitement.

definitions.definition: To be short (of or for something).
definitions.example: He'll never lack for company while he's got all that money.

definitions.definition: To be in want.
To see the deficiency in (someone or something); to find fault with, to malign, reproach.
To ward off attacks against; to fight to protect; to guard.
To support by words or writing; to vindicate, talk in favour of.
To make legal defence of; to represent (the accused).
To focus one's energies and talents on preventing opponents from scoring, as opposed to focusing on scoring.
To attempt to retain a title, or attempt to reach the same stage in a competition as one did in the previous edition of that competition.
To call a raise from the big blind.
To ward off, repel (an attack or attacker).
To prevent, to keep (from doing something).
To prohibit, forbid.
A hill, a mountain (especially in the Middle East or North Africa).
Any instrument used to gather or take by dragging; as:
Very fine mineral matter held in suspension in water.
To make a channel deeper or wider using a dredge.
To bring something to the surface with a dredge.
(Usually with up) to unearth.
definitions.example: to dredge up someone's unsavoury past

definitions.definition: Offbeat, unconventional or eccentric.
Not quite right; of questionable quality; not appropriate to the context.
Cool; great; excellent.
Having or relating to the smell of funk.
Relating to or reminiscent of various genres of African American music, especially funk.
Relating to, or characterized by, great fear, or funking.
(impolite) To emit digestive gases from the anus; to flatulate.
(usually as "fart around") To waste time with idle and inconsequential tasks; to go about one's activities in a lackadaisical manner; to be lazy or over-relaxed in one's manner or bearing.
To emit (fumes, gases, etc.).
The act of one who farts; a sound like a fart.
A tendency to be in a certain type of mood; a habitual way of thinking, behaving or reacting.
definitions.example: to have a good, bad, or calm temper

definitions.definition: State of mind; mood.
A tendency to become angry.
definitions.example: He has quite a temper when dealing with salespeople.

definitions.definition: Anger; a fit of anger.
definitions.example: an outburst of temper

definitions.definition: Calmness of mind; moderation; equanimity; composure.
definitions.example: to keep one's temper; to lose one's temper; to recover one's temper

definitions.definition: Constitution of body; the mixture or relative proportion of the four humours: blood, choler, phlegm, and melancholy.
Middle state or course; mean; medium.
The state of any compound substance which results from the mixture of various ingredients; due mixture of different qualities.
definitions.example: the temper of mortar

definitions.definition: The heat treatment to which a metal or other material has been subjected; a material that has undergone a particular heat treatment.
The state of a metal or other substance, especially as to its hardness, produced by some process of heating or cooling.
definitions.example: the temper of iron or steel

definitions.definition: (sugar manufacture) Milk of lime, or other substance, employed in the process formerly used to clarify sugar.
To moderate or control.
definitions.example: Temper your language around children.

definitions.definition: To strengthen or toughen a material, especially metal, by heat treatment; anneal.
definitions.example: Tempering is a heat treatment technique applied to metals, alloys, and glass to achieve greater toughness by increasing the strength of materials and/or ductility. Tempering is performed by a controlled reheating of the work piece to a temperature below its lower eutectic critical temperature.

definitions.definition: To sauté spices in ghee or oil to release essential oils for flavouring a dish in South Asian cuisine.
To mix clay, plaster or mortar with water to obtain the proper consistency.
To adjust, as the mathematical scale to the actual scale, or to that in actual use.
(Latinism) To govern; to manage.
To combine in due proportions; to constitute; to compose.
To mingle in due proportion; to prepare by combining; to modify, as by adding some new element; to qualify, as by an ingredient; hence, to soften; to mollify; to assuage.
To fit together; to adjust; to accommodate.
To reach a partly (or totally) unqualified conclusion.
To solve by a correct conjecture; to conjecture rightly.
definitions.example: He who guesses the riddle shall have the ring.

definitions.definition: To suppose (introducing a proposition of uncertain plausibility).
definitions.example: That album is quite hard to find, but I guess you could try ordering it online.

definitions.definition: To think, conclude, or decide (without a connotation of uncertainty). Usually in first person: "I guess".
definitions.example: "I guess I'll go to bed."

definitions.definition: To hit upon or reproduce by memory.
Of, or relating to, ontology.
Of or pertaining to the nature of being or existence.
To break or demolish something by physical bodily force.
definitions.example: The elderly woman was rescued off the floor after a young paramedic managed to kick her door down.

definitions.definition: The keeper of a personal journal, who writes in it regularly.
One whose occupation is journalism, originally only writing in the printed press.
A reporter, who professionally does living reporting on news and current events.
To yield, make or manufacture; to generate.
To make (a thing) available to a person, an authority, etc.; to provide for inspection.
To sponsor and present (a motion picture, etc) to an audience or to the public.
To extend an area, or lengthen a line.
definitions.example: to produce a side of a triangle

definitions.definition: To draw out; to extend; to lengthen or prolong.
definitions.example: to produce a man's life to threescore

definitions.definition: To alter using technology, as opposed to simply performing.
definitions.example: highly produced sound

definitions.definition: Any of various freshwater fish of the family Cyprinidae, especially the common carp, Cyprinus carpio.
A fruit of the coconut palm (not a true nut), Cocos nucifera, having a fibrous husk surrounding a large seed.
A hard-shelled seed of this fruit, having white flesh and a fluid-filled central cavity.
The edible white flesh of this fruit.
The coconut palm.
A Hispanic or dark-skinned person who acts “white” (Caucasian), alluding to the fact that a coconut is brown on the outside and white on the inside.
A black person who thinks or acts "white" (European).
A Pacific islander.
A female breast.
The human head (often used in cricket broadcasts when a ball hits or nearly hits a batsman on the head).
A carnivorous spotted large cat native to South and Central America, Panthera onca.
A place where rubbish is placed.
A business that sells used metal or items.
(1950s slang) A teenager who imitated the beatniks.

(1960s slang; still widely used in reference to that era) One who chooses not to conform to prevailing social norms: especially one who subscribes to values or actions such as acceptance or self-practice of recreational drug use, liberal or radical sexual mores, advocacy of communal living, strong pacifism or anti-war sentiment, etc.
(modern slang) A person who keeps an unkempt or sloppy appearance and wearing unusually long hair (for males), and because of it, often stereotyped as a deadbeat.
Someone who dresses in a hippie style.
One who is hip.
A footman, a liveried male servant.
A fawning, servile follower.
To attend, wait upon, serve obsequiously.
To toady, play the flunky.

A substance that is flowing, and keeping no shape, such as water; a substance of which the molecules, while not tending to separate from one another like those of a gas, readily change their relative position, and which therefore retains no definite shape, except that determined by the containing receptacle; an inelastic fluid.
definitions.example: A liquid can freeze to become a solid or evaporate into a gas.

definitions.definition: A class of consonant sounds that includes l and r.
Flowing freely like water; fluid; not solid and not gaseous; composed of particles that move freely among each other on the slightest pressure.
definitions.example: liquid nitrogen

definitions.definition: (of an asset) Easily sold or disposed of without losing value.
(of a market) Having sufficient trading activity to make buying or selling easy.
Flowing or sounding smoothly or without abrupt transitions or harsh tones.
definitions.example: a liquid melody

definitions.definition: Pronounced without any jar or harshness; smooth.
definitions.example: L and R are liquid letters.

definitions.definition: Fluid and transparent.
definitions.example: the liquid air

definitions.definition: The act of storing goods; the state of being stored.
definitions.example: There's a lot of storage space in the loft.

definitions.definition: (usually countable) An object or place in which something is stored.
(usually uncountable) Any computer device, including such as a disk, on which data is stored for a longer term than memory.
definitions.example: I′d recommend backing up these files to storage before reinstalling the operating system.

definitions.definition: The price charged for storing goods.
To put into storage; to store.
To resolve (a contest, problem, dispute, etc.); to choose, determine, or settle
definitions.example: Her last-minute goal decided the game.

definitions.definition: To make a judgment, especially after deliberation
definitions.example: I have decided that it is healthier to walk to work.

definitions.definition: To cause someone to come to a decision
To cut off; to separate
One of the simplest or essential parts or principles of which anything consists, or upon which the constitution or fundamental powers of anything are based.
definitions.example: Letters are the elements of written language.

definitions.definition: A small part of the whole.
definitions.example: an element of doubt;  an element of the picture

definitions.definition: The sky.
(with "the") Atmospheric forces such as strong winds and rains.
definitions.example: exposed to the elements

definitions.definition: A place or state of being that an individual or object is best suited to.
definitions.example: to be in one's element

definitions.definition: (usually in the plural) The bread and wine taken at Holy Communion.
A group of people within a larger group having a particular common characteristic.
definitions.example: You sometimes find the hooligan element at football matches.

definitions.definition: A component in electrical equipment, often in the form of a coil, having a high resistance, thereby generating heat when a current is passed through it.
definitions.example: The element in this electric kettle can heat the water in under a minute.

definitions.definition: One of the conceptual objects in a markup language, usually represented in text by tags.
To compound of elements.
To constitute and be the elements of.
The often ornamental coverings for an animal, especially a horse or an elephant.
Gay or rich clothing.
To dress up a horse or elephant with ornamental coverings.
To do a specific task by employing physical or mental powers.
definitions.example: He’s working in a bar.

definitions.definition: To effect by gradual degrees.
definitions.example: he worked his way through the crowd

definitions.definition: To embroider with thread.
To set into action.
definitions.example: He worked the levers.

definitions.definition: To cause to ferment.
To ferment.
To exhaust, by working.
definitions.example: The mine was worked until the last scrap of ore had been extracted.

definitions.definition: To shape, form, or improve a material.
definitions.example: He used pliers to work the wire into shape.

definitions.definition: To operate in a certain place, area, or speciality.
definitions.example: she works the night clubs

definitions.definition: To operate in or through; as, to work the phones.
To provoke or excite; to influence.
definitions.example: The rock musician worked the crowd of young girls into a frenzy.

definitions.definition: To use or manipulate to one’s advantage.
definitions.example: She knows how to work the system.

definitions.definition: To cause to happen or to occur as a consequence.
definitions.example: I cannot work a miracle.

definitions.definition: To cause to work.
definitions.example: He is working his servants hard.

definitions.definition: To function correctly; to act as intended; to achieve the goal designed for.
definitions.example: he pointed at the car and asked, "Does it work"?;  he looked at the bottle of pain pills, wondering if they would work;  my plan didn’t work

definitions.definition: To influence.
definitions.example: They worked on her to join the group.

definitions.definition: To effect by gradual degrees; as, to work into the earth.
To move in an agitated manner.
definitions.example: A ship works in a heavy sea.

definitions.definition: To behave in a certain way when handled
definitions.example: this dough does not work easily;  the soft metal works well

definitions.definition: (with two objects) To cause (someone) to feel (something); to do unto somebody (something, whether good or bad).
To hurt; to ache.
Designed or executed in a particular manner or to a particular degree.
Wrought.
Prepared so as to demonstrate the steps required.
To greet in French with "bonjour".
(in French contexts) good morning
A person whose job is to manage something, such as a business, a restaurant, or a sports team.
The head coach.
An administrator, for a singer or group.
(computer software) A window or application whose purpose is to give the user the control over some aspect of the system.
definitions.example: a file manager; a task manager; Program Manager

definitions.definition: A beast with the body of a lion (usually red), the tail of a scorpion, and the head/face of a man with a mouth filled with multiple rows of sharp teeth (like a shark), said to be able to shoot spikes from its tail or mane to paralyse prey. It may be horned, winged, or both; its voice is described as a mixture of pipes and trumpets.
A structure built or serving as an abode of human beings.
definitions.example: This is my house and my family's ancestral home.

definitions.definition: The people who live in a house; a household.
A building used for something other than a residence (typically with qualifying word).
definitions.example: On arriving at the zoo, we immediately headed for the monkey house.

definitions.definition: The audience for a live theatrical or similar performance.
A theatre.
definitions.example: After her swan-song, there wasn't a dry eye in the house.

definitions.definition: A building where a deliberative assembly meets; whence the assembly itself, particularly a component of a legislature.
definitions.example: The petition was so ridiculous that the house rejected it after minimal debate.

definitions.definition: A dynasty; a family with its ancestors and descendants, especially a royal or noble one.
definitions.example: A curse lay upon the House of Atreus.

definitions.definition: A place of rest or repose.
A grouping of schoolchildren for the purposes of competition in sports and other activities.
definitions.example: I was a member of Spenser house when I was at school.

definitions.definition: An animal's shelter or den, or the shell of an animal such as a snail, used for protection.
One of the twelve divisions of an astrological chart.
The fourth Lenormand card.
A square on a chessboard, regarded as the proper place of a piece.
The four concentric circles where points are scored on the ice.
Lotto; bingo.
A children's game in which the players pretend to be members of a household.
definitions.example: As the babysitter, Emma always acted as the mother whenever the kids demanded to play house.

definitions.definition: A small stand of trees in a swamp.
(sudoku) A set of cells in a Sudoku puzzle which must contain each digit exactly once, such as a row, column, or 3×3 box in classic Sudoku.
To remove junk from; declutter.
A short act or piece between others, as in a play; a break between acts.
Intermediate employment or time.
A pair or series of acts involving more than one person.
To act upon each other.
Tending to immerse.
Giving the impression of immersion.
A contiguous area of land, smaller than a continent, totally surrounded by water.
An entity surrounded by other entities that are very different from itself.
definitions.example: an island of colors on a butterfly's wing

definitions.definition: A superstructure on an aircraft carrier's deck.
A traffic island.
definitions.example: the island in the middle of a roundabout

definitions.definition: An unincorporated area wholly surrounded by one or more incorporated areas.
(grammar) A phrase from which a wh-word cannot be extracted without yielding invalid grammar.
To surround with water; make into an island.
To set, dot (as if) with islands.
To isolate.
One of the hairs which grow along the edge of eyelids
To calm down.
To make something loose.
definitions.example: to relax a rope or cord

definitions.definition: To become loose.
To make something less severe or tense.
definitions.example: to relax discipline

definitions.definition: To become less severe or tense.
To make something (such as codes and regulations) more lenient.
(of codes and regulations) To become more lenient.
To relieve (something) from stress.
definitions.example: Amusement relaxes the mind.

definitions.definition: To relieve from constipation; to loosen; to open.
definitions.example: An aperient relaxes the bowels.

definitions.definition: The process by which something is relaxed.
definitions.example: relaxings of the muscles

definitions.definition: Conducive to relaxation; helping one to relax.
definitions.example: a relaxing piece of music

definitions.definition: A device designed to be placed in the mouth and blown, or driven by steam or some other mechanism, to make a whistling sound.
An act of whistling.
A shrill, high-pitched sound made by whistling.
Any high-pitched sound similar to the sound made by whistling.
definitions.example: the whistle of the wind in the trees

definitions.definition: A suit (from whistle and flute).
The mouth and throat; so called as being the organs of whistling.
To make a shrill, high-pitched sound by forcing air through the mouth. To produce a whistling sound, restrictions to the flow of air are created using the teeth, tongue and lips.
definitions.example: Never whistle at a funeral.

definitions.definition: To make a similar sound by forcing air through a musical instrument or a pipe etc.
definitions.example: The stream train whistled as it passed by.

definitions.definition: To move in such a way as to create a whistling sound.
definitions.example: A bullet whistled past.

definitions.definition: To send, signal, or call by a whistle.
The explosion of a bomb or similar weapon in the air rather than on the ground.
An elevated corridor connecting an airport to a plane, usually enclosed and used for disembarking and embarking passengers and crew.
Someone or something that has a fluffy texture.
A person who is superficial, who lacks depth or seriousness.
A babycino (frothy milk drink).
Covered with fluff.
Light; soft; airy.
Warm and comforting.
Not clearly defined or explained; fuzzy.
Lightweight; superficial; lacking depth or seriousness.
A thin layer of some substance; a pellicle; a membranous covering, causing opacity.
definitions.example: a clear plastic film for wrapping food

definitions.definition: A medium used to capture images in a camera.
A movie.
Cinema; movies as a group.
A slender thread, such as that of a cobweb.
To record (activity, or a motion picture) on photographic film.
definitions.example: A Hollywood studio was filming on location in NYC.

definitions.definition: To cover or become covered with a thin skin or pellicle.
The quality of being amusing, comical, funny.
definitions.example: She has a great sense of humour, and I always laugh a lot whenever we get together.

definitions.definition: A mood, especially a bad mood; a temporary state of mind or disposition brought upon by an event; an abrupt illogical inclination or whim.
definitions.example: He was in a particularly vile humour that afternoon.

definitions.definition: Any of the fluids in an animal body, especially the four "cardinal humours" of blood, yellow bile, black bile and phlegm that were believed to control the health and mood of the human body.
Either of the two regions of liquid within the eyeball, the aqueous humour and vitreous humour.
Moist vapour, moisture.
To pacify by indulging.
definitions.example: I know you don't believe my story, but humour me for a minute and imagine it to be true.

definitions.definition: Litre, liter
Pound (sterling) (from Latin libra)
Something that is easy
Comfortable; at ease.
definitions.example: Now that I know it's taken care of, I can rest easy at night.

definitions.definition: Requiring little skill or effort.
definitions.example: It's often easy to wake up but hard to get up.

definitions.definition: Causing ease; giving comfort, or freedom from care or labour.
definitions.example: Rich people live in easy circumstances.

definitions.definition: Free from constraint, harshness, or formality; unconstrained; smooth.
definitions.example: easy manners; an easy style

definitions.definition: (of a woman) Consenting readily to sex.
definitions.example: She has a reputation for being easy; they say she slept with half the senior class.

definitions.definition: Not making resistance or showing unwillingness; tractable; yielding; compliant.
Not straitened as to money matters; opposed to tight.
definitions.example: The market is easy.

definitions.definition: In a relaxed or casual manner.
definitions.example: After his illness, John decided to take it easy.

definitions.definition: In a manner without strictness or harshness.
definitions.example: Jane went easier on him after he broke his arm.

definitions.definition: Used an intensifier for large magnitudes.
definitions.example: This project will cost 15 million dollars, easy.

definitions.definition: Not difficult, not hard.
To stop rowing.
Lacking in force (usually strength) or ability.
definitions.example: The child was too weak to move the boulder.

definitions.definition: Unable to sustain a great weight, pressure, or strain.
definitions.example: a weak timber; a weak rope

definitions.definition: Unable to withstand temptation, urgency, persuasion, etc.; easily impressed, moved, or overcome; accessible; vulnerable.
definitions.example: weak resolutions; weak virtue

definitions.definition: Dilute, lacking in taste or potency.
definitions.example: We were served stale bread and weak tea.

definitions.definition: (grammar) Displaying a particular kind of inflection, including:
That does not ionize completely into anions and cations in a solution.
definitions.example: a weak acid;  a weak base

definitions.definition: One of the four fundamental forces associated with nuclear decay.
Bad or uncool.
definitions.example: This place is weak.

definitions.definition: Having a narrow range of logical consequences; narrowly applicable. (Often contrasted with a strong statement which implies it.)
Resulting from, or indicating, lack of judgment, discernment, or firmness; unwise; hence, foolish.
Not having power to convince; not supported by force of reason or truth; unsustained.
definitions.example: The prosecution advanced a weak case.

definitions.definition: Lacking in vigour or expression.
definitions.example: a weak sentence; a weak style

definitions.definition: Not prevalent or effective, or not felt to be prevalent; not potent; feeble.
Tending towards lower prices.
definitions.example: a weak market; wheat is weak at present

definitions.definition: Lacking contrast.
definitions.example: a weak negative

definitions.definition: Any species of fish of the family Tetraodontidae that have the ability to inflate themselves to a globe several times their normal size by swallowing water or air when threatened.
A delicacy popular in Japan, consisting of the fish served raw as sushi or perhaps fried. It may, if improperly prepared, contain considerably deadly levels of neurotoxins.
Any of several animals related to Equus ferus caballus.
Equipment with legs.
Type of equipment.

A mass of earthy matter, or rock of the same character as the wall rock, occurring in the course of a vein, as of coal or ore; hence, to take horse (said of a vein) is to divide into branches for a distance.
The sedative, antidepressant, and anxiolytic drug morphine, chiefly when used illicitly.

An informal variant of basketball in which players match shots made by their opponent(s), each miss adding a letter to the word "horse", with 5 misses spelling the whole word and eliminating a player, until only the winner is left. Also HORSE, H-O-R-S-E or H.O.R.S.E. (see Variations of basketball#H-O-R-S-E).
(among students) A translation or other illegitimate aid in study or examination.
(among students) horseplay; tomfoolery

An aircraft that is borne along by one or more sets of long rotating blades which allow it to hover, move in any direction including reverse, or land; and typically having a smaller set of blades on its tail that stabilize the aircraft.
definitions.example: We flew over the city in a helicopter.

definitions.definition: A powered troweling machine with spinning blades used to spread concrete.
The winged fruit of certain trees, such as ash, elm, and maple.
A dragonfly.
A whirling trick performed with devil sticks.
To transport by helicopter.
To travel by helicopter.
To rotate like a helicopter blade.
definitions.example: helicoptering his jacket, helicoptering his arms

definitions.definition: To overprotect one's children.
An experience of something in advance.
An advance showing of a film, exhibition etc.
Something seen in advance.
A facility for seeing and checking a document, or changes to it, before saving and/or printing it.
To show or watch something, or part of it, before it is complete.
A master of Tibetan Buddhism.
A toad of species Bufo calamita (or Epidalea calamita), known for its running lizard-like gait and very loud mating call.
An aircraft powered by three jet engines.
Three jets of particles from an event or source
A number of things or persons being in some relation to one another.
definitions.example: A group of people gathered in front of the Parliament to demonstrate against the Prime Minister's proposals.

definitions.definition: A set with an associative binary operation, under which there exists an identity element, and such that each element has an inverse.
An effective divisor on a curve.
A (usually small) group of people who perform music together.
definitions.example: Did you see the new jazz group?

definitions.definition: A small number (up to about fifty) of galaxies that are near each other.
A column in the periodic table of chemical elements.
A functional group.
definitions.example: Nitro is an electron-withdrawing group.

definitions.definition: A subset of a culture or of a society.
An air force formation.
A collection of formations or rock strata.
A number of users with same rights with respect to accession, modification, and execution of files, computers and peripherals.
An element of an espresso machine from which hot water pours into the portafilter.
A number of eighth, sixteenth, etc., notes joined at the stems; sometimes rather indefinitely applied to any ornament made up of a few short notes.
A set of teams playing each other in the same division, while not during the same period playing any teams that belong to other sets in the division.
A commercial organization.
To put together to form a group.
definitions.example: group the dogs by hair colour

definitions.definition: To come together to form a group.
An utterance of goodbye, the wishing of farewell to someone.
definitions.example: They made their good-byes.

definitions.definition: To say goodbye; to wish somebody farewell on parting.
Farewell; a formula used to another person or persons when the speaker, writer, or person addressed is departing.
Rapid or incoherent talk, with indistinct utterance; gibberish.
To talk rapidly, indistinctly, or unintelligibly; to utter gibberish or nonsense.
To utter rapidly or indistinctly; to gabble.
A human being, whether man, woman or child.
definitions.example: Humans share common ancestors with other apes.

definitions.definition: To behave as or become, or to cause to behave as or become, a human.
(notcomp) Of or belonging to the species Homo sapiens or its closest relatives.
Having the nature or attributes of a human being.
definitions.example: To err is human; to forgive, divine.

definitions.definition: The state or quality of being perplexed; puzzled or confused.
Something that perplexes.
In information theory, a measurement of how well a probability distribution or model predicts a sample.
A state of tranquility, quiet, and harmony; absence of violence. For instance, a state free from civil disturbance.
definitions.example: Our lounge strives to maintain an environment of peace for the comfort of our customers.

definitions.definition: A state free of oppressive and unpleasant thoughts and emotions.
definitions.example: The safety equipment will give me some peace of mind.

definitions.definition: Harmony in personal relations.
A state free of war, in particular war between different countries.
definitions.example: May there be peace in our time.

definitions.definition: To make peace; to put at peace; to be at peace.
To peace out.
Shut up!, silence!; be quiet, be silent.
Shortened form of peace out; goodbye.
A female member of a royal family other than a queen, especially a daughter or granddaughter.
A woman or girl who excels in a given field or class.
A female ruler or monarch; a queen.
The wife of a prince; the female ruler of a principality.
definitions.example: Princess Grace was the Princess of Monaco.

definitions.definition: A young girl; used as a term of endearment.
A young girl or woman (or less commonly a man) who is vain, spoiled or selfish; a prima donna.
A tinted crystal marble used in children's games.
A type of court card in the Tarot pack, coming between the 10 and the prince (Jack).
A female lemur.
An act of dodging.
A trick, evasion or wile.
A line of work.
To avoid (something) by moving suddenly out of the way.
definitions.example: He dodged traffic crossing the street.

definitions.definition: To avoid; to sidestep.
definitions.example: The politician dodged the question with a meaningless reply.

definitions.definition: To go hither and thither.
(videography) To decrease the exposure for certain areas of an image in order to make them darker (compare burn).
To follow by dodging, or suddenly shifting from place to place.
To trick somebody.
Dodgy
A mythical creature reputed to be mischievously inclined to damage or dismantle machinery.
(by extension) Any mysterious, unknown source of trouble or mischief.
definitions.example: We rechecked everything, and we suspect gremlins in the database.

definitions.definition: A young inexperienced surfer.
To make dizzy or unsteady.
To reel; to whirl.
Dizzy, feeling dizzy or unsteady and as if about to fall down.
definitions.example: The man became giddy upon standing up so fast.

definitions.definition: Causing dizziness: causing dizziness or a feeling of unsteadiness.
definitions.example: They climbed to a giddy height.

definitions.definition: Lightheartedly silly, or joyfully elated.
definitions.example: The boy was giddy when he opened his birthday presents.

definitions.definition: Frivolous, impulsive, inconsistent, changeable.
To sway back and forth; to totter or reel.
definitions.example: Flowers wavered in the breeze.

definitions.definition: To flicker, glimmer, quiver, as a weak light.
To fluctuate or vary, as commodity prices or a poorly sustained musical pitch.
To shake or tremble, as the hands or voice.
definitions.example: His voice wavered when the reporter brought up the controversial topic.

definitions.definition: To falter; become unsteady; begin to fail or give way.
To be indecisive between choices; to feel or show doubt or indecision; to vacillate.
definitions.example: Despite all the terrible things that happened to her, she never wavered from her beliefs.

definitions.definition: A state of fluctuation or indecision.
definitions.example: the waverings of politicians, trying to please everybody

definitions.definition: Fluctuating; being in doubt; undetermined; indecisive; uncertain; unsteady.
The brother or brother-in-law of one’s parent.
The male cousin of one’s parent.
A companion to one's (usually unmarried) mother.
A source of advice, encouragement, or help.
A pawnbroker.
An affectionate term for a man of an older generation than oneself, especially a friend of one's parents, by means of fictive kin.
An older male African-American person.
To address somebody by the term uncle.
To act like, or as, an uncle.
A cry used to indicate surrender.
In company; together.
definitions.example: John played the piano and everyone sang along.

definitions.definition: Onward, forward, with progressive action.
definitions.example: Don't stop here. Just move along.

definitions.definition: By the length of; in a line with the length of; lengthwise next to.
In a line with, with a progressive motion on; onward on; forward on.
A state or situation of great confusion, disorder, trouble or destruction; chaos.
definitions.example: In all the mayhem, some children were separated from their partners.

definitions.definition: Infliction of violent injury on a person or thing.
definitions.example: The fighting dogs created mayhem in the flower beds.

definitions.definition: The maiming of a person by depriving him of the use of any of his limbs which are necessary for defense or protection.
The crime of damaging things or harming people on purpose.
To question harshly in an attempt to find or reveal weaknesses.
To insult, tease, make fun of or badger.
definitions.example: Promise that you won't heckle me after my performance.

definitions.definition: To prepare flax for spinning using special combs called hackles
A tool for chopping.
A hacking blow.
A gouge or notch made by such a blow.
A dry cough.
A hacking; a catch in speaking; a short, broken cough.
A try, an attempt.
The foothold traditionally cut into the ice from which the person who throws the rock pushes off for delivery.
A mattock or a miner's pickaxe.
An expedient, temporary solution, such as a small patch or change to code, meant to be replaced with a more elegant solution at a later date.
An interesting technical achievement, particularly in computer programming.
A trick, shortcut, skill, or novelty method to increase productivity, efficiency or ease.
definitions.example: Putting your phone in a sandwich bag when you go to the beach is such a great hack.

definitions.definition: An illegal attempt to gain access to a computer network.
A video game or any computer software that has been altered from its original state.
Time check.
A swing of the bat at a pitched ball by the batter.
definitions.example: He took a few hacks, but the pitcher finally struck him out.

definitions.definition: A kick on the shins in football.
Confinement of an officer to their stateroom as a punishment.
To chop or cut down in a rough manner.
definitions.example: They hacked the brush down and made their way through the jungle.

definitions.definition: To cough noisily.
definitions.example: This cold is awful. I can't stop hacking.

definitions.definition: To withstand or put up with a difficult situation.
definitions.example: Can you hack it out here with no electricity or running water?

definitions.definition: To make a quick code change to patch a computer program, often one that, while being effective, is inelegant or makes the program harder to maintain.
definitions.example: I hacked in a fix for this bug, but we'll still have to do a real fix later.

definitions.definition: To accomplish a difficult programming task.
definitions.example: He can hack like no one else and make the program work as expected.

definitions.definition: To work with something on an intimately technical level.
definitions.example: I'm currently hacking distributed garbage collection.

definitions.definition: (by extension) To apply a trick, shortcut, skill, or novelty method to something to increase productivity, efficiency or ease.
definitions.example: I read up on dating tips so I can hack my sex life.

definitions.definition: To hack into; to gain unauthorized access to (a computer system, e.g., a website, or network) by manipulating code.
(by extension) To gain unauthorised access to a computer or online account belonging to (a person or organisation).
definitions.example: When I logged into the social network, I discovered I'd been hacked.

definitions.definition: To strike an opponent's leg with one's hockey stick.
definitions.example: He's going to the penalty box after hacking the defender in front of the goal.

definitions.definition: To make a flailing attempt to hit the puck with a hockey stick.
definitions.example: There's a scramble in front of the net as the forwards are hacking at the bouncing puck.

definitions.definition: To swing at a pitched ball.
definitions.example: He went to the batter's box hacking.

definitions.definition: (soccer and rugby) To kick (a player) on the shins.
To strike in a frantic movement.
To strike lightly as part of tapotement massage.
To have (a smile) on one's face.
definitions.example: I don't know what he's smiling about.

definitions.definition: To express by smiling.
definitions.example: to smile consent, or a welcome

definitions.definition: To express amusement, pleasure, or love and kindness.
To look cheerful and joyous; to have an appearance suited to excite joy.
definitions.example: The sun smiled down from a clear summer sky.

definitions.definition: To be propitious or favourable; to countenance.
definitions.example: The gods smiled on his labours.

definitions.definition: The action of the verb to smile.
definitions.example: Smiling takes fewer muscles than frowning.

definitions.definition: That smiles or has a smile.
definitions.example: smiling children

definitions.definition: A swirling body of water.
definitions.example: A whirlpool is an instance of a vortex produced by ocean tides, or by a hole underneath where the water would drain out, such as in a bathtub.

definitions.definition: A hot tub, jacuzzi.
Turmoil, or agitated excitement.
To spin or swirl like the water in a whirlpool.
Promoting good physical health and well-being.
Promoting moral and mental well-being.
Favourable to morals, religion or prosperity; sensible; conducive to good; salutary; promoting virtue or being virtuous.
Marked by wholeness; sound and healthy.
One who acts in a silly or ridiculous fashion; a clown or fool.
An unintentionally ridiculous person.
To behave like a buffoon
The room where a group of people (especially the board of a company or organization) conducts its meetings
Corporations or corporate management considered as a section of society
definitions.example: Though the new law is popular among the general public, it is hated in the boardroom.

definitions.definition: Strong affection.
A person who is the object of romantic feelings; a darling, a sweetheart, a beloved.
A term of friendly address, regardless of feelings.
definitions.example: Hello love, how can I help you?

definitions.definition: A thing, activity etc which is the object of one's deep liking or enthusiasm.
Sexual desire; attachment based on sexual attraction.
Sexual activity.
An instance or episode of being in love; a love affair.
Used as the closing, before the signature, of a letter, especially between good friends or family members, or by the young.
Alternative letter-case form of Love.
A thin silk material.
A climbing plant, Clematis vitalba.
(usually transitive, sometimes intransitive, stative) To have a strong affection for (someone or something).
definitions.example: I love my spouse.   I love you!

definitions.definition: To need, thrive on.
definitions.example: Mold loves moist, dark places.

definitions.definition: To be strongly inclined towards something; an emphatic form of like.
definitions.example: I love walking barefoot on wet grass;  I'd love to join the team;  I love what you've done with your hair

definitions.definition: (usually transitive, sometimes intransitive) To care deeply about, to be dedicated to (someone or something).
To derive delight from a fact or situation.
definitions.example: I love the fact that the coffee shop now offers fat-free chai latte.

definitions.definition: To have sex with (perhaps from make love).
definitions.example: I wish I could love her all night long.

definitions.definition: A nervous wriggling or twitching motion.
A person who fidgets, especially habitually.
A toy intended to be fidgeted with.
To wiggle or twitch; to move around nervously or idly.
To cause to fidget; to make uneasy.
To cease from action, motion, work, or performance of any kind; stop; desist; be without motion.
To come to a pause or an end; end.
To be free from that which harasses or disturbs; be quiet or still; be undisturbed.
To be or to put into a state of rest.
definitions.example: I shall not rest until I have uncovered the truth.

definitions.definition: To stay, remain, be situated.
definitions.example: The blame seems to rest with your father.

definitions.definition: To lean, lie, or lay.
definitions.example: A column rests on its pedestal.

definitions.definition: To complete one's active advocacy in a trial or other proceeding, and thus to wait for the outcome (however, one is still generally available to answer questions, etc.)
definitions.example: I rest my case.

definitions.definition: To sleep; slumber.
To lie dormant.
To sleep the final sleep; sleep in death; die; be dead.
To rely or depend on.
definitions.example: The decision rests on getting a bank loan.

definitions.definition: To be satisfied; to acquiesce.
To remain.
To arrest.
The action of rest
A place where one can rest; a resting-place.
A pause; a break; an interlude.
That rests; that is not in action or in the process of growth.
Unemployed; out of work.
Remaining; stationary.
A racquet: an implement with a handle connected to a round frame strung with wire, sinew, or plastic cords, and used to hit a ball, such as in tennis or a birdie in badminton.
A snowshoe formed of cords stretched across a long and narrow frame of light wood.
A broad wooden shoe or patten for a man or horse, to allow walking on marshy or soft ground.
To strike with, or as if with, a racket.
To use a gaff, especially to land a fish.
To cheat or hoax.
To gamble.
To affix or cover with gaffer tape.
The process of landing a fish with a gaff.
A person who believes; especially regarding religion.
definitions.example: She's a believer in life after death.

definitions.definition: The act of killing.
definitions.example: The assassin liked to make a clean kill, and thus favored small arms over explosives.

definitions.definition: Specifically, the death blow.
definitions.example: The hunter delivered the kill with a pistol shot to the head.

definitions.definition: The result of killing; that which has been killed.
definitions.example: The fox dragged its kill back to its den.

definitions.definition: The grounding of the ball on the opponent's court, winning the rally.
To put to death; to extinguish the life of.
definitions.example: Smoking kills more people each year than alcohol and drugs combined.

definitions.definition: To render inoperative.
definitions.example: He killed the engine and turned off the headlights, but remained in the car, waiting.

definitions.definition: To stop, cease or render void; to terminate.
definitions.example: My computer wouldn't respond until I killed some of the running processes.

definitions.definition: To amaze, exceed, stun or otherwise incapacitate.
definitions.example: That joke always kills me.

definitions.definition: To cause great pain, discomfort or distress to.
definitions.example: These tight shoes are killing my feet.

definitions.definition: To produce feelings of dissatisfaction or revulsion in.
definitions.example: It kills me to learn how many poor people are practically starving in this country while rich moguls spend such outrageous amounts on useless luxuries.

definitions.definition: To use up or to waste.
definitions.example: He told the bartender, pointing at the bottle of scotch he planned to consume, "Leave it, I'm going to kill the bottle."

definitions.definition: To exert an overwhelming effect on.
definitions.example: Between the two of us, we killed the rest of the case of beer.

definitions.definition: To overpower, overwhelm or defeat.
definitions.example: The team had absolutely killed their traditional rivals, and the local sports bars were raucous with celebrations.

definitions.definition: To force a company out of business.
To produce intense pain.
definitions.example: You don't ever want to get rabies. The doctor will have to give you multiple shots and they really kill.

definitions.definition: To punish severely.
definitions.example: My parents are going to kill me!

definitions.definition: To strike (a ball, etc.) with such force and placement as to make a shot that is impossible to defend against, usually winning a point.
To cause (a ball, etc.) to be out of play, resulting in a stoppage of gameplay.
To succeed with an audience, especially in comedy.
To cause to assume the value zero.
(IRC) To disconnect (a user) involuntarily from the network.
To deadmelt.
(north-east US) A creek; a body of water; a channel or arm of the sea.
definitions.example: Schuylkill, Catskill, etc.

definitions.definition: A kiln.
The part of the body connecting the head and the trunk found in humans and some animals.
definitions.example: Giraffes have long necks.

definitions.definition: The corresponding part in some other anatomical contexts.
The part of a shirt, dress etc., which fits a person's neck.
The tapered part of a bottle toward the opening.
The slender tubelike extension atop an archegonium, through which the sperm swim to reach the egg.
The extension of any stringed instrument on which a fingerboard is mounted
A long narrow tract of land projecting from the main body, or a narrow tract connecting two larger tracts.
A reduction in size near the end of an object, formed by a groove around it.
definitions.example: a neck forming the journal of a shaft

definitions.definition: The constriction between the root and crown of a tooth.
The gorgerin of a capital.
The small part of a gun between the chase and the swell of the muzzle.
A person's life.
definitions.example: to risk one's neck; to save someone's neck

definitions.definition: A falsehood; a lie.
Shapeshifting water spirits in Germanic mythology and folklore
To hang by the neck; strangle; kill, eliminate
definitions.example: Go neck yourself.

definitions.definition: To make love; to intently kiss or cuddle; to canoodle.
definitions.example: Alan and Betty were necking in the back of a car when Betty's dad caught them.

definitions.definition: To drink rapidly.
To decrease in diameter.
That which has a distinct existence as an individual unit. Often used for organisations which have no physical form.
The existence of something considered apart from its properties.
Anything about which information or data can be stored in a database; in particular, an organised array or set of individual elements or parts.
The state or quality of being or existence.
definitions.example: The group successfully maintains its tribal entity.

definitions.definition: A spirit, ghost, or the like.
An alien lifeform that has no corporeal body.
A condition of total, interlocking traffic congestion on the streets or highways of a crowded city, in which no one can move because everyone is in someone else's way.

On a smaller scale: the situation in which cars enter a signal-controlled intersection too late during the green light cycle, and are unable to clear the intersection (due to congestion in the next block) when the light turns red, thus blocking the cross traffic when it's their turn to go. Repeated at enough intersections, this phenomenon can lead to citywide gridlock.
(by extension) any paralysis of a complex system due to severe congestion, conflict, or deadlock.
To cause traffic congestion
Full of kinks; liable to kink or curl.
definitions.example: kinky hair

definitions.definition: Marked by unconventional sexual preferences or behavior, as fetishism, sadomasochism, and other sexual practices.
Queer; eccentric; crotchety.
A looping of a piece of string or of any other long, flexible material that cannot be untangled without passing one or both ends of the material through its loops.
definitions.example: Climbers must make sure that all knots are both secure and of types that will not weaken the rope.

definitions.definition: (of hair, etc) A tangled clump.
definitions.example: The nurse was brushing knots from the protesting child's hair.

definitions.definition: A maze-like pattern.
A non-self-intersecting closed curve in (e.g., three-dimensional) space that is an abstraction of a knot (in sense 1 above).
definitions.example: A knot can be defined as a non-self-intersecting broken line whose endpoints coincide: when such a knot is constrained to lie in a plane, then it is simply a polygon.

definitions.definition: A difficult situation.
definitions.example: I got into a knot when I inadvertently insulted a policeman.

definitions.definition: The whorl left in lumber by the base of a branch growing out of the tree's trunk.
definitions.example: When preparing to tell stories at a campfire, I like to set aside a pile of pine logs with lots of knots, since they burn brighter and make dramatic pops and cracks.

definitions.definition: Local swelling in a tissue area, especially skin, often due to injury.
definitions.example: Jeremy had a knot on his head where he had bumped it on the bedframe.

definitions.definition: A protuberant joint in a plant.
Any knob, lump, swelling, or protuberance.
The swelling of the bulbus glandis in members of the dog family, Canidae
The point on which the action of a story depends; the gist of a matter.
definitions.example: the knot of the tale

definitions.definition: A node.
A kind of epaulet; a shoulder knot.
A group of people or things.
A bond of union; a connection; a tie.

A unit of speed, equal to one nautical mile per hour. (From the practice of counting the number of knots in the log-line (as it is paid out) in a standard time. Traditionally spaced at one every 1/120 of a mile.)
definitions.example: Cedric claimed his old yacht could make 12 knots.

definitions.definition: A nautical mile
The bulbus glandis
In omegaverse fiction, a bulbus glandis-like structure on the penis of a male alpha, which ties him to an omega during intercourse.
To form into a knot; to tie with a knot or knots.
definitions.example: We knotted the ends of the rope to keep it from unravelling.

definitions.definition: To form wrinkles in the forehead, as a sign of concentration, concern, surprise, etc.
definitions.example: She knotted her brow in concentration while attempting to unravel the tangled strands.

definitions.definition: To unite closely; to knit together.
To entangle or perplex; to puzzle.
To form knots.
To knit knots for a fringe.
One of a variety of shore birds; the red-breasted sandpiper (variously Calidris canutus or Tringa canutus).
One who steals from the pocket of a passerby, usually by sleight of hand.
To pick pockets; to steal.
To make something fluffy.
definitions.example: The cat fluffed its tail.

definitions.definition: To become fluffy, puff up.
To move lightly like fluff.
(of an actor or announcer) To make a mistake in one's lines.
To do incorrectly, for example mishit, miskick, miscue etc.
To fart.
To arouse (a male pornographic actor) before filming.
An esoteric doctrine or treatise; esoteric philosophy.
One who believes, or is an initiate, in esoteric doctrines or rites.
Intended for or likely to be understood by only a small number of people with a specialized knowledge or interest, or an enlightened inner circle.
definitions.example: The writing in this manual is very esoteric; I’d need a degree in engineering just to understand it!

definitions.definition: Having to do with concepts that are highly theoretical and without obvious practical application; often with mystical or religious connotations.
Confidential; private.
definitions.example: an esoteric meeting

definitions.definition: Having great size, or great strength, force or power.
A board game for two players with each beginning with sixteen chess pieces moving according to fixed rules across a chessboard with the objective to checkmate the opposing king.
A heavy yellow elemental metal of great value, with atomic number 79 and symbol Au.
A coin or coinage made of this material, or supposedly so.
A deep yellow colour, resembling the metal gold.
The bullseye of an archery target.
A gold medal.
definitions.example: France has won three golds and five silvers.

definitions.definition: Anything or anyone that is very valuable.
(in the plural) A grill (jewellery worn on front teeth) made of gold.
To pyrolyze or burn food until the color begins to change to a light brown, but not as dark as browning
Made of gold.
Having the colour of gold.
(of commercial services) Premium, superior.
Below the legal age for some activity, such as drinking or having sex.
Not yet a legal adult; still a minor.
